verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce autem in Ephraim orta est seditio : nam transeuntes contra aquilonem, dixerunt ad Jephte : Quare vadens ad pugnam contra filios Ammon, vocare nos noluisti, ut pergeremus tecum ? igitur incendemus domum tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּצָּעֵק֙ אִ֣ישׁ אֶפְרַ֔יִם וַֽיַּעֲבֹ֖ר צָפ֑וֹנָה וַיֹּאמְר֨וּ לְיִפְתָּ֜ח מַדּ֣וּעַ ׀ עָבַ֣רְתָּ ׀ לְהִלָּחֵ֣ם בִּבְנֵי־עַמּ֗וֹן וְלָ֙נוּ֙ לֹ֤א קָרָ֙אתָ֙ לָלֶ֣כֶת עִמָּ֔ךְ בֵּיתְךָ֕ נִשְׂרֹ֥ף עָלֶ֖יךָ בָּאֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: But behold there arose a sedition in Ephraim. And passing towards the north, they said to Jephte: When thou wentest to fight against the children of Ammon, why wouldst thou not call us, that we might go with thee? Therefore we will burn thy house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ephraimin miehet tulivat kokoon ja menivät pohjoiseen päin ja sanoivat Jephtalle: miksis menit sotaan Ammonin lapsia vastaan, ja et meitä kutsunut menemään kanssas? Me poltamme sinun ja sinun huonees.
verses.king_james: And the men of Ephraim gathered themselves together, and went northward, and said unto Jephthah, Wherefore passedst thou over to fight against the children of Ammon, and didst not call us to go with thee? we will burn thine house upon thee with fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quibus ille respondit : Disceptatio erat mihi et populo meo contra filios Ammon vehemens : vocavique vos, ut præberetis mihi auxilium, et facere noluistis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יִפְתָּח֙ אֲלֵיהֶ֔ם אִ֣ישׁ רִ֗יב הָיִ֛יתִי אֲנִ֛י וְעַמִּ֥י וּבְנֵֽי־עַמּ֖וֹן מְאֹ֑ד וָאֶזְעַ֣ק אֶתְכֶ֔ם וְלֹא־הוֹשַׁעְתֶּ֥ם אוֹתִ֖י מִיָּדָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he answered them: I and my people were at great strife with the children of Ammon: and I called you to assist me, and you would not do it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jephta sanoi heille: minulla ja minun kansallani oli suuri riita Ammonin lasten kanssa, ja minä kutsuin teitä, ja ette auttaneet minua heidän käsistänsä.
verses.king_james: And Jephthah said unto them, I and my people were at great strife with the children of Ammon; and when I called you, ye delivered me not out of their hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod cernens, posui animam meam in manibus meis, transivique ad filios Ammon, et tradidit eos Dominus in manus meas. Quid commerui, ut adversum me consurgatis in prælium ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶרְאֶ֞ה כִּֽי־אֵינְךָ֣ מוֹשִׁ֗יעַ וָאָשִׂ֨ימָה נַפְשִׁ֤י בְכַפִּי֙ וָֽאֶעְבְּרָה֙ אֶל־בְּנֵ֣י עַמּ֔וֹן וַיִּתְּנֵ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה בְּיָדִ֑י וְלָמָ֞ה עֲלִיתֶ֥ם אֵלַ֛י הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּ֖ה לְהִלָּ֥חֶם בִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when I saw this, I put my life in my own hands, and passed over against the children of Ammon and the Lord delivered them into my hands. What have I deserved, that you should rise up to fight against me?
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska minä näin, ettette tahtoneet vapahtaa minua, panin minä sieluni minun käteeni ja menin Ammonin lapsia vastaan, ja Herra antoi heidän minun käteeni: miksi te tulitte minun tyköni tänäpänä sotimaan minua vastaan?
verses.king_james: And when I saw that ye delivered me not, I put my life in my hands, and passed over against the children of Ammon, and the Lord delivered them into my hand: wherefore then are ye come up unto me this day, to fight against me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocatis itaque ad se cunctis viris Galaad, pugnabat contra Ephraim : percusseruntque viri Galaad Ephraim, quia dixerat : Fugitivus est Galaad de Ephraim, et habitat in medio Ephraim et Manasse.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְבֹּ֤ץ יִפְתָּח֙ אֶת־כׇּל־אַנְשֵׁ֣י גִלְעָ֔ד וַיִּלָּ֖חֶם אֶת־אֶפְרָ֑יִם וַיַּכּוּ֩ אַנְשֵׁ֨י גִלְעָ֜ד אֶת־אֶפְרַ֗יִם כִּ֤י אָֽמְרוּ֙ פְּלִיטֵ֤י אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ אַתֶּ֔ם גִּלְעָ֕ד בְּת֥וֹךְ אֶפְרַ֖יִם בְּת֥וֹךְ מְנַשֶּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then calling to him all the men of Galaad, he fought against Ephraim: and the men of Galaad defeated Ephraim, because he had said: Galaad is a fugitive of Ephraim, and dwelleth in the midst of Ephraim and Manasses.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jephta kokosi kaikki Gileadin miehet ja soti Ephraimia vastaan, ja Gileadin miehet löivät Ephraimin, että he sanoivat: oletteko te Gileadilaiset Ephraimin ja Manassen seassa, te Ephraimin karkurit.
verses.king_james: Then Jephthah gathered together all the men of Gilead, and fought with Ephraim: and the men of Gilead smote Ephraim, because they said, Ye Gileadites are fugitives of Ephraim among the Ephraimites, and among the Manassites.

verses.latin_vulgate: Occupaveruntque Galaaditæ vada Jordanis, per quæ Ephraim reversurus erat. Cumque venisset ad ea de Ephraim numero, fugiens, atque dixisset : Obsecro ut me transire permittatis : dicebant ei Galaaditæ : Numquid Ephrathæus es ? quo dicente : Non sum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּלְכֹּ֥ד גִּלְעָ֛ד אֶֽת־מַעְבְּר֥וֹת הַיַּרְדֵּ֖ן לְאֶפְרָ֑יִם וְֽ֠הָיָ֠ה כִּ֣י יֹאמְר֞וּ פְּלִיטֵ֤י אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ אֶֽעֱבֹ֔רָה וַיֹּ֨אמְרוּ ל֧וֹ אַנְשֵֽׁי־גִלְעָ֛ד הַאֶפְרָתִ֥י אַ֖תָּה וַיֹּ֥אמֶֽר ׀ לֹֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Galaadites secured the fords of the Jordan, by which Ephraim was to return. And when any one of the number of Ephraim came thither in the flight, and said: I beseech you let me pass: the Galaadites said to him: Art thou not an Ephraimite? If he said: I am not:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Gileadilaiset ennättivät Jordanin luotuspaikat Ephraimin edestä. Koska ne paenneet Ephraimista sanoivat: anna minun käydä ylitse, niin sanoivat Gileadin miehet hänelle: oletkos Ephraimilainen? Ja hän vastasi: en.
verses.king_james: And the Gileadites took the passages of Jordan before the Ephraimites: and it was so, that when those Ephraimites which were escaped said, Let me go over; that the men of Gilead said unto him, Art thou an Ephraimite? If he said, Nay;

verses.latin_vulgate: interrogabant eum : Dic ergo Scibboleth, quod interpretatur Spica. Qui respondebat : Sibboleth : eadem littera spicam exprimere non valens. Statimque apprehensum jugulabant in ipso Jordanis transitu. Et ceciderunt in illo tempore de Ephraim quadraginta duo millia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמְרוּ לוֹ֩ אֱמׇר־נָ֨א שִׁבֹּ֜לֶת וַיֹּ֣אמֶר סִבֹּ֗לֶת וְלֹ֤א יָכִין֙ לְדַבֵּ֣ר כֵּ֔ן וַיֹּאחֲז֣וּ אוֹת֔וֹ וַיִּשְׁחָט֖וּהוּ אֶל־מַעְבְּר֣וֹת הַיַּרְדֵּ֑ן וַיִּפֹּ֞ל בָּעֵ֤ת הַהִיא֙ מֵֽאֶפְרַ֔יִם אַרְבָּעִ֥ים וּשְׁנַ֖יִם אָֽלֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: They asked him: Say then, Scibboleth, which is interpreted, An ear of corn. But he answered, Sibboleth, not being able to express an ear of corn by the same letter. Then presently they took him and killed him in the very passage of the Jordan. And there fell at that time of Ephraim, two and forty thousand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin he käskivät hänen sanoa: Sjibbolet, niin hän sanoi Sibbolet, ja ei taitanut sitä niin sanoa; niin ottivat he hänen kiinni ja tappoivat hänen Jordanin luotuspaikalla, niin että silloin lankesi Ephraimista kaksiviidettäkymmentä tuhatta.
verses.king_james: Then said they unto him, Say now Shibboleth: and he said Sibboleth: for he could not frame to pronounce it right. Then they took him, and slew him at the passages of Jordan: and there fell at that time of the Ephraimites forty and two thousand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Judicavit itaque Jephte Galaadites Israël sex annis : et mortuus est, ac sepultus in civitate sua Galaad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֥ט יִפְתָּ֛ח אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שֵׁ֣שׁ שָׁנִ֑ים וַיָּ֗מׇת יִפְתָּח֙ הַגִּלְעָדִ֔י וַיִּקָּבֵ֖ר בְּעָרֵ֥י גִלְעָֽד׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Jephte, the Galaadite, judged Israel six years: and he died, and was buried in his city of Galaad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jephta tuomitsi Israelia kuusi ajastaikaa; ja Jephta Gileadilainen kuoli ja haudattiin Gileadin kaupunkeihin.
verses.king_james: And Jephthah judged Israel six years. Then died Jephthah the Gileadite, and was buried in one of the cities of Gilead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hunc judicavit Israël Abesan de Bethlehem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֤ט אַֽחֲרָיו֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אִבְצָ֖ן מִבֵּ֥ית לָֽחֶם׃
verses.douay_rheims: After him Abesan of Bethlehem judged Israel:
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämän jälkeen tuomitsi Ibtsan Betlehemistä Israelia.
verses.king_james: And after him Ibzan of Beth–lehem judged Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui habuit triginta filios, et totidem filias, quas emittens foras, maritis dedit, et ejusdem numeri filiis suis accepit uxores, introducens in domum suam. Qui septem annis judicavit Israël :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי־ל֞וֹ שְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים בָּנִ֗ים וּשְׁלֹשִׁ֤ים בָּנוֹת֙ שִׁלַּ֣ח הַח֔וּצָה וּשְׁלֹשִׁ֣ים בָּנ֔וֹת הֵבִ֥יא לְבָנָ֖יו מִן־הַח֑וּץ וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֥ט אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שֶׁ֥בַע שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: He had thirty sons, and as many daughters, whom he sent abroad, and gave to husbands, and took wives for his sons, of the same number, bringing them into his house. And he judged Israel seven years:
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänellä oli kolmekymmentä poikaa, ja kolmekymmentä tytärtä hän naitti ulos, ja kolmekymmentä tytärtä otti hän muukalaisista pojillensa; ja tuomitsi Israelia seitsemän ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And he had thirty sons, and thirty daughters, whom he sent abroad, and took in thirty daughters from abroad for his sons. And he judged Israel seven years.

verses.latin_vulgate: mortuusque est, ac sepultus in Bethlehem.
verses.douay_rheims: And he died, and was buried in Bethlehem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ibtsan kuoli ja haudattiin Betlehemissä.
verses.king_james: Then died Ibzan, and was buried at Beth–lehem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui successit Ahialon Zabulonites : et judicavit Israël decem annis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֤ט אַֽחֲרָיו֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֵיל֖וֹן הַזְּבוּלֹנִ֑י וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֥ט אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל עֶ֥שֶׂר שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: To him succeeded Ahialon, a Zabulonite: and he judged Israel ten years:
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämän jälkeen tuomitsi Israelia Elon Sebulonilainen, ja hän tuomitsi Israelia kymmenen ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And after him Elon, a Zebulonite, judged Israel; and he judged Israel ten years.

verses.latin_vulgate: mortuusque est, ac sepultus in Zabulon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֖מׇת אֵיל֣וֹן הַזְּבוּלֹנִ֑י וַיִּקָּבֵ֥ר בְּאַיָּל֖וֹן בְּאֶ֥רֶץ זְבוּלֻֽן׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he died, and was buried in Zabulon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elon Sebulonilainen kuoli ja haudattiin Ajalonissa, Sebulonin maalla.
verses.king_james: And Elon the Zebulonite died, and was buried in Aijalon in the country of Zebulun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hunc judicavit Israël Abdon filius Illel Pharathonites :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֥ט אַחֲרָ֖יו אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל עַבְדּ֥וֹן בֶּן־הִלֵּ֖ל הַפִּרְעָתוֹנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: After him, Abdon, the son of Illel, a Pharathonite, judged Israel:
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämän jälkeen tuomitsi Israelia Abdon Hillelin poika, Pirtagonilainen.
verses.king_james: And after him Abdon the son of Hillel, a Pirathonite, judged Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui habuit quadraginta filios, et triginta ex eis nepotes, ascendentes super septuaginta pullos asinarum. Et judicavit Israël octo annis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי־ל֞וֹ אַרְבָּעִ֣ים בָּנִ֗ים וּשְׁלֹשִׁים֙ בְּנֵ֣י בָנִ֔ים רֹכְבִ֖ים עַל־שִׁבְעִ֣ים עֲיָרִ֑ם וַיִּשְׁפֹּ֥ט אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל שְׁמֹנֶ֥ה שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he had forty sons, and of them thirty grandsons, mounted upon seventy ass colts, and he judged Israel eight years:
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänellä oli neljäkymmentä poikaa ja kolmekymmentä pojanpoikaa, jotka ajoivat seitsemälläkymmenellä aasin varsalla; hän tuomitsi Israelia kahdeksan ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: And he had forty sons and thirty nephews, that rode on threescore and ten ass colts: and he judged Israel eight years.

verses.latin_vulgate: mortuusque est, ac sepultus in Pharathon terræ Ephraim, in monte Amalec.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֛מׇת עַבְדּ֥וֹן בֶּן־הִלֵּ֖ל הַפִּרְעָתוֹנִ֑י וַיִּקָּבֵ֤ר בְּפִרְעָתוֹן֙ בְּאֶ֣רֶץ אֶפְרַ֔יִם בְּהַ֖ר הָעֲמָלֵקִֽי׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he died, and was buried in Pharathon, in the land of Ephraim, in the mount of Amalech.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Abdon Hillelin poika, Pirtagonilainen, kuoli ja haudattiin Pirtagoniin Ephraimin maalla, Amalekilaisten vuorella.
verses.king_james: And Abdon the son of Hillel the Pirathonite died, and was buried in Pirathon in the land of Ephraim, in the mount of the Amalekites.
